title: Corollary 'exact-slice': vanishing over PROPER H uses wrong threshold (r_H vs r_G)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap (specific to the current revised solution)

Corollary `cor:exact-slice` claims $\Sigma^k HM$ is **exactly** an $\mathcal O$-$(kr_G)$-slice, where $r_G=[G:\chi^{\mathcal O}(G)]$. The upper-truncation half requires that ALL generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ map trivially into $\Sigma^k HM$, i.e. for every $H\subseteq G$ and every admissible $T\in\mathcal O(H)$ with $|T|>kr_G$,
$$[G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1,\ \Sigma^kHM]^G \cong [N^TS^1,\ i_H^*\Sigma^kHM]^H = 0.$$

The proof dispatches this in one sentence: "By Lemma `lem:restr`(1)-(2) and Lemma `lem:geom`(3) applied over each $H$ (with $i_H^*\Sigma^k HM$, which is again of the form $\Sigma^k H(\text{geometric})$ over $H$), all maps from these generators into $\Sigma^k HM$ vanish." This is **not justified** and is likely false as stated:

1. **Geometricity does not restrict.** Lemma `lem:geom`(3) requires the target to be $\Sigma^k H M'$ with $M'$ a *geometric* Mackey functor for $(H,\mathcal O|_H)$, meaning (G1) $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-local and (G2) $\Phi^H(\,\cdot\,)$ an EM spectrum in degree 0. The hypothesis on $M$ is at the $G$-level: $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local and $\Phi^G HM$ EM in degree 0. There is no argument that $i_H^*HM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-local (the family of proper subgroups changes when passing to $H$) nor that $\Phi^H i_H^*HM$ is concentrated in degree 0. In fact $\Phi^H i_H^* HM$ can be a nonzero, non-EM spectrum.

2. **Threshold mismatch $r_H$ vs $r_G$.** Even granting geometricity over $H$, Lemma `lem:geom`(3) at $H$ gives vanishing only for $|T|>k\,r_H$ with $r_H=[H:\chi^{\mathcal O|_H}(H)]=[H:\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)]$ (Lemma `lem:restr`(1)). But the truncation $P^{kr_G}_{\mathcal O}$ provides cells only with $|T|>k r_G$. When $H$ is a proper subgroup with $r_H<r_G$ (e.g. any proper $H$ with $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)=H$, so $r_H=1$), the threshold $kr_G+1$ is FAR above $kr_H$, so the available constraint $|T|>kr_G$ does not even reach the regime $kr_H<|T|\le kr_G$ where $i_H^*\Sigma^kHM$ could have nonzero slices. The cited lemma simply does not apply at the needed degree.

## Why this matters
Corollary `cor:exact-slice` is invoked implicitly to justify that the geometric Postnikov layers $\Sigma^j HM_j$ are honest slices and that the filtration in the converse is the slice filtration. If the exactness fails at proper $H$, the identification of $\Sigma^kHM$ as a $(kr_G)$-slice is unproven, undermining the claim that the constructed tower is the $\mathcal O$-slice tower (as opposed to merely a tower with the right connectivity).

## Difficulty
This is the hardest genuine gap. Closing it requires either (i) proving that for proper $H$, $i_H^*\Sigma^kHM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P_H}$-locally trivial or $\Phi^H$-trivial (so that no induced generator at proper $H$ maps in nontrivially), or (ii) reorganizing so that exactness of slices is never needed at proper $H$. A concrete acid test: verify the corollary directly for $G=C_{p^2}$, $\mathcal O=\{C_p\to C_{p^2}\}$ (so $\chi(G)=C_p$, $r_G=p$, but $r_{C_p}=1$), where the mismatch is maximal.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review against the current `solution.tex`.

In the current text the proper-$H$ case of `cor:exact-slice` is handled via $i_H^*HM\simeq 0$ (correct, since $M$ is geometric so $(i_H^*M)(H/K)=M(G/K)=0$ for $K\subseteq H$ proper), giving $[N^TS^1,i_H^*\Sigma^kHM]^H=0$ for free. So the 'wrong threshold' problem is no longer present. The only residual is that nullity is verified on generators (and their suspensions) rather than propagated to all of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$ via the (o1)--(o3) closure already proven in `lem:cover-conn`. Suggest closing/merging into a minor presentation note (and note this corollary is a bonus, not used by the main theorem).

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution

I reviewed the current `solution.tex` against this issue and confirm the flagged gap is **resolved**; I tightened the relevant passage for full rigor.

### Why the original gap is gone
The earlier proof of `cor:exact-slice` dispatched the proper-$H'$ case by trying to apply geometricity and `lem:geom`(3) *over $H'$*. That is exactly what the issue (correctly) objected to:
1. geometricity does not restrict (so `lem:geom`(3)'s hypotheses fail over $H'$), and
2. the threshold from `lem:geom`(3) over $H'$ is $|T|>k r_{H'}$, which for proper $H'$ with $r_{H'}<r_G$ does not reach the available constraint $|T|>k r_G$.

The current proof avoids BOTH problems by using the strictly stronger fact that the **target** restricts to zero at proper $H'$:
$$ i_{H'}^\ast HM \simeq 0 \quad\text{for every proper } H'\subsetneq G. $$
This holds because $i_{H'}^\ast$ is $t$-exact, so $i_{H'}^\ast HM$ is again an EM $H'$-spectrum with $\underline\pi_0 = i_{H'}^\ast M$, and $(i_{H'}^\ast M)(H'/J)=M(G/J)=0$ for all $J\subseteq H'\subsetneq G$ since $J$ is then proper in $G$ and $M$ is geometric (M1). Hence $i_{H'}^\ast HM$ is the EM spectrum of the zero Mackey functor, i.e. contractible. This is precisely the computation already carried out in `prop:geomEM`(G1). Therefore
$$[N^{T'\sqcup\ell\ast}S^1,\ i_{H'}^\ast W]^{H'}=0$$
holds outright, with no $H'$-level connectivity/geometric threshold and no degree mismatch. Only the $H'=G$ case invokes `lem:geom`(3), where the threshold is genuinely $r_G$ and matches.

### Acid test ($G=C_{p^2}$, $\mathcal O=\{C_p\to C_{p^2}\}$)
Here $\chi(G)=C_p$, $r_G=p$, $r_{C_p}=1$. Proper subgroups are $e$ and $C_p$. For geometric $M$: $M(G/e)=M(G/C_p)=0$, so $i_{C_p}^\ast HM\simeq0$ and no induced generator from $H'=C_p$ contributes. The maximal mismatch the issue highlights is thus circumvented, not bridged.

### Propagation to all of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}$
The residual concern (nullity on generators vs. the whole subcategory) is also fully handled in the current text: the proof introduces the class
$$\mathcal N_W=\{X : [\Sigma^\ell(G/K_+\otimes X),W]^G=0\ \forall K\subseteq G,\ \ell\ge0\},$$
proves it is closed under (o1)â€“(o3) and contains the generating set $\mathcal G$, hence $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge kr_G+1}=\langle\mathcal G\rangle_{\ge}\subseteq\mathcal N_W$, giving $[X,\Sigma^kHM]^G=0$ for all such $X$ (take $K=G,\ell=0$).

### Edit made
I rewrote the "Case $H'\subsetneq G$ proper" paragraph in `cor:exact-slice` to state and prove $i_{H'}^\ast HM\simeq0$ explicitly (via $t$-exactness of $i_{H'}^\ast$ and (M1)), and to spell out that we deliberately do NOT use `lem:geom`(3) over $H'$, so the $r_{H'}$-vs-$r_G$ mismatch never arises.

Numerically/logically sanity-checked the restriction identity and the acid test. The corollary's proof is now rigorous and the gap is closed.
